127069.explanation: Westland Sensible believes that citizen initiatives must be facilitated faster and better by the municipality of Westland.

127071.explanation: Westland is very densely built-up, also in the villages. Extension of opening hours comes at the expense of the neighborhood. We want to prevent that.

127072.explanation: The municipality must devise schemes to create opportunities for our citizens that make having solar panels or heat pumps easier. We are thinking of subsidy options and rental of solar panels.

127073.explanation: New distribution centers mean a shift of shopping public outside the current shopping centers. We cherish our shopping centers, which play an important role in Westland for relaxation, fun shopping, and hospitality.

127074.explanation: The Municipality of Westland already accommodates 2,300 displaced people from Ukraine and additionally each year more than 200 recognized refugees. Add to that 5,000 labor migrants who are housed and the severe housing shortage among our own residents, then no space can be found for asylum seekers in Westland.

127075.explanation: Fine dust produced by fireplaces and stoves hinders many residents of Westland in their living environment.

127078.explanation: Within the waste disposal levy, it must be possible to offer this facility to our residents. Furthermore, this prevents dumping and keeps Westland clean.

127080.explanation: The municipality should financially assist with new construction and at least prevent that extra facilities are charged to the new residents. For existing buildings, a subsidy scheme should provide better accessibility.

127082.explanation: Besides school swimming, attention should be paid to culture in education. We therefore support making music lessons compulsory and coordinating this with the various school boards.

127085.explanation: To remove annoyances among residents, more enforcement officers (BOAs) are needed. There are many complaints about fatbikes in the villages, which make the centers unsafe. There are also many complaints about illegal parking, speeding, and trucks ignoring truck bans. Enforcement must therefore be stronger.

127086.explanation: Our residents want more enforcement on the Westland roads. There are too many traffic accidents in Westland. That is why there must be more control. The police are insufficient, so more cameras must be installed.

127087.explanation: The domestic violence shelter already exists at the Vreeburglaan from Perspectief. Such facilities are needed, as well as homes for protected living and homes for residents who are unable to live in their own social housing.

127088.explanation: If we want to take enforcement officers (BOAs) seriously, we should also equip them with the means available to enforce rules.

127091.explanation: Given the housing shortage, all avenues must be explored to create affordable homes for starters and young families. If conversions are only possible with subsidies, the municipality should in principle provide financial support.

127092.explanation: Although there are still few students in Westland, we interpret the question as referring to young Westlanders who study elsewhere and still live with their parents and want to live independently. Temporary housing is needed for that, and the municipality must invest in it.

127094.explanation: Westland prides itself on being a great area to live and do business. It is known that many Westlanders are very financially well off. However, there are also people who have to get by on a very low income. We refer to the many residents who only receive an AOW pension.

127095.explanation: Westland Sensible supports raising the income limit for benefits such as the Child Package and special social assistance. We want to set them all at 130 percent of the social assistance standard.

127096.explanation: In Westland, there is quite a bit of nuisance from youths who hang around everywhere. Youth work plays an important role in solving the nuisance as well as in prevention. There must be no cuts to this.

127098.explanation: 30 km/h means a shorter braking distance which benefits traffic safety. More accidents occur in Westland than in most other municipalities, hence this measure.

127101.explanation: Care is already difficult to organize, and this requirement would create an extra barrier. We do not want that, aside from the principled aspect of the choice.

127102.explanation: Of course, quality must be assessed first. Given the rising costs of youth care, the financial aspect must also be monitored. After all, money can only be spent once.

127106.explanation: But then in the right place. That place is in Westland along the A20 towards Rotterdam. In Westland itself we want no more expansions than the business parks because of the housing and limited accessibility due to the too old road structure.

127108.explanation: Our Westland residents who have long been registered for a home should not be disadvantaged in favor of newly registered recognized refugees. That is not fair and cannot be explained.

127111.explanation: No child should leave primary school without a swimming diploma. The municipality must make this financially possible.

127116.explanation: Westland Sensible supports the hospitation scheme, provided that the existing scheme for housing labor migrants in existing homes in the villages remains in place. So no dozens of labor migrants in one terraced house.

127119.explanation: There is currently a shortage that must be solved quickly. Not only in Naaldwijk but also in Monster, Wateringen and other villages there is a great need for parking spaces, especially on Fridays and Saturdays.

127121.explanation: We want to continue in the current existing building. Many residents find it a cozy and pleasant theater and do not want a theater many times larger. Many residents also find the €110,000,000 too much and think that money can be better spent on roads, bicycle paths and sidewalks.

127123.explanation: Especially around Wateringen, 's-Gravenzande, Poeldijk and Kwintsheul new roads must be built to relieve the overly busy traffic. In Naaldwijk we already have the Piet Struikweg, in De Lier the Eastern Ring Road, and between Kwintsheul and Wateringen the Van Luijkweg.

127126.explanation: The current recycling centers are poorly located and especially on Saturdays, given the use of the adjacent sports fields, there are queues. Furthermore, the current site can be well used for senior housing. A modern recycling center that is easily accessible must therefore be built.

127127.explanation: Westland is growing, and if needed, facilities such as sports fields must grow along with it.

127069.explanation: GBW believes in a small government and a strong community. Residents know their environment best and can carry out tasks more efficiently, cheaply, and with greater involvement.

127071.explanation: Entrepreneurs themselves know best what works. Fewer rules increase freedom and economic dynamism.

127072.explanation: Initiative is good, but subsidies should only be given if there is local benefit and it is financially responsible.

127073.explanation: Space for both economy and livability. No ideological blockades, but tailor-made solutions per location.

127074.explanation: Westland is full. Municipalities should not be disproportionately burdened without support.

127075.explanation: Freedom of residents is paramount. No additional prohibitions as long as health is not seriously harmed.

127078.explanation: Free services lead to misuse and higher costs. Personal responsibility is fairer.

127080.explanation: Entrepreneurs decide on investments themselves. The municipality should not subsidize everything.

127082.explanation: Valuable, but not a core municipal task.

127085.explanation: Visible enforcement increases safety and livability.

127086.explanation: An effective tool against crime, provided it is applied carefully.

127087.explanation: Protection of the vulnerable is a core government task.

127088.explanation: Safety is important, but proportionality and training are decisive.

127091.explanation: The market can often handle this itself. Subsidies distort fair competition.

127092.explanation: Westland is not a student city; focus on local housing needs.

127094.explanation: Everyone contributes. Targeted help is better than generic exemption.

127095.explanation: Assistance should remain focused on those who truly need it.

127096.explanation: Prevention prevents bigger social problems later.

127098.explanation: Safety and traffic flow require tailored solutions per neighborhood.

127101.explanation: Quality and availability take precedence over identity.

127102.explanation: Affordability is necessary to keep care accessible.

127106.explanation: Room for employment and economic growth.

127108.explanation: Equal treatment for all housing seekers.

127111.explanation: Important, but primarily the responsibility of parents and schools to implement.

127116.explanation: Balance between housing pressure and livability.

127119.explanation: Accessibility is crucial for the economy.

127123.explanation: Improve traffic flow without unnecessary hardening.

127126.explanation: Only if necessary and the location is appropriate.

127127.explanation: Sport promotes health and social cohesion.

127069.explanation: CDA Westland supports residents who want to take municipal tasks into their own hands, for example, neighborhood green maintenance. We support residents and citizen initiatives who organize themselves in this regard.

127071.explanation: CDA Westland listens to the needs of entrepreneurs and takes into account the residents of village centers. The current rules accommodate both interests. Entrepreneurs are largely satisfied with this.

127072.explanation: CDA Westland supports further sustainability efforts in our municipality. We back existing subsidy schemes and initiatives to encourage people to become more sustainable, such as installing cable tray tiles for charging electric cars and the fix brigade for people with low incomes.

127073.explanation: Westland already has many business parks. CDA Westland believes there is no room for new distribution centers that have nothing to do with greenhouse horticulture. Additionally, Honderdland in Maasdijk has been completed recently, and a decision has been made to expand ABC Westland in Poeldijk.

127074.explanation: Westland already accommodates relatively more Ukrainian refugees and status holders than surrounding municipalities. Furthermore, our municipality must house many labor migrants. CDA Westland believes we must contribute appropriately. A large-scale asylum seeker center does not fit here.

127075.explanation: CDA Westland believes the municipality should not impose additional regulations here. Residents must consider each other and adhere to national advice regarding wood burning.

127078.explanation: CDA Westland wants to maintain the costs for bulky waste collection (€17.50 per pick-up). Residents can still go to the municipal yard for free.

127080.explanation: CDA Westland believes that employers, associations, and volunteer organizations should be encouraged to make their organizations open and accessible. The municipality can assist in cooperation with Platform Gehandicapten Westland (PGW).

127082.explanation: CDA Westland is in favor of the current forms of music lessons. In cooperation with Westland Cultuurweb, we encourage the municipality to support and expand music education.

127085.explanation: CDA Westland wants more police presence on the streets. Therefore, we support the mayor's efforts for more neighborhood officers and enforcement officers. The municipality must have sufficient enforcement officers and neighborhood police who supervise the neighborhoods and village centers and are approachable for residents.

127086.explanation: CDA Westland wants to use targeted camera surveillance more often. We make it possible to have extra supervision, cameras, and enforcement in places where there is frequent nuisance.

127087.explanation: Domestic violence is a large and growing problem. People must always be able to find a safe place. CDA Westland believes we should properly arrange this in our region through cooperation with Veilig Thuis Haaglanden and the exploration of other initiatives.

127088.explanation: CDA Westland supports more safety means for enforcement officers (boa's). The option to carry a baton must be available. We want the municipality to investigate under what conditions this can be implemented.

127091.explanation: CDA Westland strongly supports transforming commercial buildings and offices into housing. This should happen in locations where companies no longer want to settle.

127092.explanation: CDA Westland supports creating a flexible housing buffer in Westland. These homes can be used for various target groups, such as separated individuals.

127094.explanation: CDA Westland favors minimizing the cancellation of municipal taxes. In Westland, this is currently only done for waste disposal and sewage charges. We keep an eye on the human aspect and continue supporting people living in poverty.

127095.explanation: CDA Westland believes that poverty among Westlanders should not persist long-term. Therefore, the municipality must support residents. This can, for example, be through helping find meaningful work.

127096.explanation: CDA Westland supports youth work by Vitis Welzijn. They are an indispensable link in countering nuisance caused by loitering youth and guide young people in their work and education. Youth workers can also timely detect emerging criminal behavior.

127098.explanation: CDA Westland opposes the standard lowering of the maximum speed limit. Traffic flow is also important. We will continue to pay attention to unsafe traffic situations.

127101.explanation: CDA Westland believes people should have free choice of their care provider.

127102.explanation: CDA Westland believes that the quality of youth care is most important. We also pay attention to the costs involved.

127106.explanation: CDA Westland opposes establishing more industrial estates in Westland. In recent years, Honderdland in Maasdijk has been completed and approval has been given for the expansion of ABC Westland in Poeldijk. Therefore, over the next four years, we first want a vision for our industrial estates.

127108.explanation: CDA Westland believes the municipality itself should decide the rules about who gets priority for social housing and when. Westland is best suited to determine this. We adhere to our provincial task for the reception of status holders.

127111.explanation: CDA Westland supports making school swimming available to people with low incomes through the Child Package.

127116.explanation: CDA Westland wants to encourage renting out entire homes as much as possible to maintain overview and keep parking issues to a minimum. In certain circumstances, however, we should consider whether subdividing entire houses can provide a solution.

127119.explanation: CDA Westland recognizes that parking in Naaldwijk is already often challenging during the day and requires extra attention. Solutions must be found by creating more parking spaces. We do this within the broader developments of the 'Verdi area'.

127121.explanation: CDA Westland ensures the already established plans for the new construction of WestlandTheater De Naald proceed. The new building ensures that Westlanders can continue to enjoy culture and film.

127123.explanation: CDA Westland sees that there is increasingly less space in Westland to build more roads. We support the continued improvement of traffic flow, for example by reorganizing roads or adding extra asphalt.

127126.explanation: CDA Westland believes that the current two recycling centers offer sufficient options to dispose of bulky waste for free. There is no need to open an additional recycling center.

127127.explanation: CDA Westland supports the expansion of sports parks. We do this in good consultation with our sports clubs. Recently, we have done this when deciding on the expansion of sports parks in De Lier and Naaldwijk.

127069.explanation: Taxes for management have already been paid. The municipality must simply manage this properly.

127071.explanation: There should be generous closing hours, but there should be no uncontrolled proliferation that could cause nuisance to nearby residents.

127073.explanation: Westland is a busy municipality where the distribution of fruits and vegetables is very important.

127074.explanation: Asylum issues must be addressed at the source and not shifted onto municipalities. A strict admission and return policy for illegal residence.

127075.explanation: There is already enough paternalism in the Netherlands.

127078.explanation: This is an extra effort and must be possible, but at actual costs (perception costs).

127080.explanation: The entrepreneur should determine, depending on the business model, whether this facility is needed.

127082.explanation: The schools must facilitate this themselves.

127085.explanation: There is primarily a need for more police officers on the street.

127091.explanation: Provided the location and target group meet the desired standards. Westland youth and starters should have priority.

127094.explanation: After all, you have an income. If it is at social assistance level, it might be possible.

127098.explanation: Only on roads where it also makes sense in terms of design.

127102.explanation: Attention must be paid to measurable quality.

127106.explanation: As far as these can be integrated into public spaces.

127108.explanation: Westland's own residents should always have priority.

127111.explanation: Very important in our municipality with many waterways and bordering the North Sea.

127116.explanation: Converting single-family homes into multiple units leads to distressing situations.

127119.explanation: They should have been there already. But in 2009 they were expertly cut short by parties that shot down the Verdilaan center plan.

127121.explanation: The costs of maintaining the already dilapidated building are sky-high. Westland now has 120,000 inhabitants and needs a good new theater.

127126.explanation: There are already plans for this.

127127.explanation: Very necessary as the number of residents increases.

127069.explanation: This is easier said than done, and besides, you can't compare a public park to a sports hall. The operation of a sports hall often takes place outside the municipality. Managing public parks yourself is already possible in some cases and we should encourage this if residents want it.

127071.explanation: The Westland VVD supports extending opening hours, which are determined by the municipality. Entrepreneurs can then decide for themselves whether they want to make use of these extended opening hours.

127072.explanation: If the municipality of Westland wants to make progress on sustainability, residents must also be supported in this.

127073.explanation: There is no place in Westland for storage locations of, for example, Bol.com or Zalando.

127074.explanation: Westland is a densely built and populated municipality, there is a severe shortage of housing, there is an increasing need for business space, and we all want to recreate in a green environment. There is no place for an asylum seekers' center.

127075.explanation: A cozy fire must remain possible and the nanny state should not go too far.

127078.explanation: In Westland we have well-accessible municipal recycling centers. These are free and should remain so.

127080.explanation: The Westland VVD finds it important that people with disabilities can fully participate in society. However, entrepreneurs themselves also have a role here.

127082.explanation: Every child deserves to develop their talents, and these opportunities are available through the Westland Pass and Child Package for those who cannot afford it. However, choices must be made and not everyone can do everything.

127085.explanation: Safety begins close to home and the Westland VVD wants more BOAs who are visible and approachable in the neighborhood.

127086.explanation: Police and enforcement officers unfortunately cannot be present everywhere all the time. Therefore, the Westland VVD believes that in places with long-term nuisance and where the sense of security is affected, for example areas with high crime, it is a good alternative.

127087.explanation: Violence has a tremendous impact on someone's life. It is therefore important that someone can remain in their own familiar environment. The potential establishment of such a home must be done in consultation with surrounding municipalities.

127088.explanation: BOAs must feel safe and be able to defend themselves (if necessary).

127091.explanation: Solutions such as converting old office buildings into homes should be possible. The municipality is expected to take a proactive role, which does not mean it has to pay for everything. In addition, there is also a large demand for office space in Westland.

127092.explanation: The Westland VVD wants to focus on creative solutions to tackle the current shortage, and as far as we're concerned, this doesn't have to be only for students.

127094.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes is not fair.

127095.explanation: Work must pay off and we do not encourage this by raising the income limit to receive poverty assistance.

127096.explanation: Investing in youth work now can prevent worse (and more expensive) problems later. Youth work is preventive, involves young people, and prevents more serious problems in later life. Of course, this must be done as effectively and efficiently as possible.

127098.explanation: In areas where traffic safety is at risk, this can be a solution. However, the Westland VVD is not in favor of applying this everywhere.

127101.explanation: Appropriate care is important, but availability and quality are leading.

127102.explanation: Price is a factor, but quality is at least as important.

127106.explanation: We must give our entrepreneurs space. There is great demand for business parks and we must continue to facilitate that, but this must be balanced with, for example, the housing task.

127108.explanation: It cannot be the case that a Westland resident has been on a housing waiting list for years while a refugee with a residence permit immediately gets a home.

127111.explanation: It is important that every child can swim in a municipality by the sea.

127116.explanation: This undermines the livability in a neighborhood.

127119.explanation: However, this applies not only to Naaldwijk but also to other village centers.

127121.explanation: The current theater is at the end of its lifespan. Renovation measures are not cost-effective in the long term. That is why the Westland VVD wants to opt for a new theater in which we can plan ahead for over 50 years.

127123.explanation: More homes are being added and if no roads are built, accessibility and flow will suffer.

127126.explanation: There are enough recycling centers in Westland.

127127.explanation: Sports facilities must grow with the number of inhabitants of Westland. It is important that everyone who wants to play sports can do so and is not held back by waiting lists.

127069.explanation: Self-management of parks by the neighborhood already leads to an improvement in the quality and appearance of parks. Taking over the management of municipal buildings, such as a sports hall, must also be possible under clear conditions.

127071.explanation: Entrepreneurs can be given more flexibility in their opening hours, but they should not be allowed to decide this entirely themselves. Extension of opening hours must be done in consultation with the municipality and the surroundings.

127072.explanation: This is the ideal picture: residents jointly generate their own green and affordable electricity with solar panels. They use the electricity in the same place, so the grid is not burdened.

127073.explanation: Too many huge distribution centers have been built in the Netherlands in recent years that spoil the landscape. Small-scale distribution centers focused on the Westland economy and greenhouse horticulture can be allowed under conditions.

127074.explanation: Due to the dispersion law, the municipality is obliged to contribute to accommodating asylum seekers. By informing residents well from the start and setting clear conditions, this is also feasible according to GroenLinks-PvdA Westland.

127075.explanation: Wood burning causes relatively a lot of nuisance for the surroundings and should be reduced. Especially at times when the smoke lingers for a long time, it should be prohibited.

127078.explanation: Trash, old mattresses, and other litter on the streets are annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. Making disposal completely free actually increases the clutter on the streets. The current small financial contribution as a price incentive works better in this regard.

127080.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. However, the obligation to make entrepreneurs' buildings accessible lies with the entrepreneurs themselves. The municipality can assist entrepreneurs with information about possible steps.

127082.explanation: Self-development is important for children, and music can contribute to that. GroenLinks-PvdA Westland wants every primary school student to receive a number of free music lessons with various instruments to discover if it suits them.

127085.explanation: The number of enforcement officers has recently already been increased. We want to see if this expansion is sufficient for Westland and, if necessary, further scale up the number of enforcement officers.

127086.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But more cameras are not the right solution in our opinion. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be violated lightly. Therefore, we prefer more street enforcement officers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

127087.explanation: If you no longer feel safe at home, there must be a place you can always go to. A shelter for domestic violence victims is indispensable for our municipality.

127088.explanation: The monopoly on the use of force must remain with the police, who are also trained to handle it carefully.

127091.explanation: There is also a shortage of office space in Westland, and office buildings are often located where a good living environment cannot be sufficiently guaranteed. In places where this is feasible, the costs for the transformation should be borne by the developer.

127092.explanation: With the development of the Flora Campus, the number of students is expected to increase. They should also be able to live within Westland. The Flora Campus plans also include space for student housing. The municipality must closely monitor whether this housing is sufficient.

127094.explanation: If debt forgiveness of municipal taxes stops, people with the lowest incomes in our municipality will get into big trouble. They simply cannot miss that money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water, which is why we support debt forgiveness.

127095.explanation: We see an increase in people living in poverty who just miss qualifying for poverty alleviation programs. Therefore, we want to raise the income threshold so that more people become eligible.

127096.explanation: Youth work ensures that young people are less likely to go down the wrong path and increases the number who later become productive members of society. Investments in youth work save money in the long term for social assistance and adult care.

127098.explanation: The data clearly shows: a lower speed limit makes traffic in our municipality MUCH safer. For cycling children and elderly people with mobility issues, this makes a world of difference. Only separated access roads may have a 50 km/h limit.

127101.explanation: Free choice of a care provider where a care recipient feels comfortable is important. People's preferences must be well taken into account.

127102.explanation: The waiting lists and problems in youth assistance and youth care are enormous. You don’t solve this by cutting more. We must primarily ensure certainty and offer help that matches the concerns of our youth. The quality and accessibility of care must not suffer from financial incentives.

127106.explanation: With the current sites and those already under development, Westland should manage for the time being. It is important to keep an eye on this and timely develop new business parks if future developments require it.

127108.explanation: The municipality must fulfill its obligation to house status holders. If priority for a social rental home is really necessary, then that option must be available.

127111.explanation: The Netherlands is a water country and every child should learn to swim, including children whose parents cannot afford swimming lessons. Unfortunately, this is still not fairly arranged. School swimming is quite a burden in terms of time and organization for schools, so it should not become mandatory.

127116.explanation: We have a huge housing shortage and many people in our municipality live more spaciously than actually needed. Therefore, we want to make it easier for people to rent out individual rooms in their own homes, provided they are well maintained and the rent is fair.

127119.explanation: Currently, the number of parking spaces in the center of Naaldwijk meets the demand. We prefer promoting bicycle use for going out and small shopping trips.

127121.explanation: The theater in its current form has reached its limit and no longer meets guidelines and regulations. Thoroughly renovating the current theater is also expensive and would reduce seating in the main hall by 70 seats. We choose the only option to keep film and theater accessible.

127123.explanation: Westland must ensure accessibility is in order and remains so. Population growth automatically requires investments in infrastructure. A balance must be sought where public transport, cycling, and walking are full alternatives to car use.

127126.explanation: The design and accessibility of the current recycling center leave much to be desired and cannot be resolved at the current location.

127127.explanation: Sports parks must grow along with demand. With a rising population, the number of athletes also increases. The municipality is already proactively addressing this, with announced expansions in De Lier, Naaldwijk, and Honselersdijk. This policy should continue into the future.

127069.explanation: Working together on the municipality contributes to strong cohesion.

127071.explanation: Depending on the situation and impact on society. What some need can cause nuisance to others.

127072.explanation: Support from the municipality in implementation where possible, but not financially.

127073.explanation: If we opt to expand the logistics sector, then only along the Westland Route or A20, focusing on automation and processing of our own products.

127074.explanation: D66 supports a nationwide distribution in the reception of asylum seekers and care for people fleeing war or insecurity. This also means small-scale reception in Westland. A challenge to realize but also our responsibility.

127075.explanation: Where we encourage residents to take the RIVM's Burning Guide into account.

127078.explanation: The current system of a small contribution (€12.50) works sufficiently. The costs for residents are limited but make the system cost-covering.

127080.explanation: Responsibility and choice lie with the entrepreneur. A subsidy scheme is a possible interim solution.

127082.explanation: Children should come into contact with music, dance, art, and other forms of expression via schools. Together and under professional guidance. D66 Westland stands for expanding the programs of Cultuurweb Westland. Not individually, but discovering together.

127085.explanation: Enforcement only partially contributes to the feeling of safety. D66 Westland believes there are enough enforcement officers. There is room to invest in other matters that increase the feeling of safety, for example, the quality of public spaces and street lighting.

127086.explanation: The use of camera surveillance is bound by strict rules. Politics has little say over this. D66 Westland supports camera surveillance if necessary. At the moment, there is no reason for it. If necessary, consider temporary or permanent use.

127087.explanation: There is a women's shelter in Westland. This must be preserved. Besides shelter for women, solutions must be worked on to remove the perpetrator from the situation, for example by a restraining order. This way, victims and children can stay in their own environment.

127091.explanation: Vacancy should be discouraged but at the expense of those who leave it vacant. The municipality does not finance solving long-term vacancy.

127092.explanation: This problem does not occur for students here, only for labor migrants.

127094.explanation: A good arrangement that we want to maintain and further research its implementation.

127095.explanation: Residents approaching the poverty line can be helped earlier to prevent them from falling into poverty. Faster help is better for both the resident and the municipality.

127096.explanation: Youth work is important in early detection and prevention of future problems among and with youth. We do not want to lose this valuable role.

127098.explanation: The traffic situation is fundamental. Generally, 30 km/h is the standard, but other considerations can be made for safety or to prevent shortcut traffic. The municipality made these decisions in 2025.

127101.explanation: Basically, everyone should have a choice. In practice, this will not always be possible.

127102.explanation: Quality must come first. Investing in prevention prevents high future costs in further healthcare provision.

127106.explanation: D66 supports targeted expansions that strengthen and expand the economic character of Westland, such as the Flora Campus. For expansion of the logistics sector, this should only be along the Westland route or A20, focusing on automation and processing of local products.

127108.explanation: We take responsibility for housing status holders. The goal is to fairly distribute the available social rental housing and spread status holders throughout the municipality.

127111.explanation: There is no school swimming. The municipality ensures sufficient swimming water. For residents who need financial support, arrangements are available to still offer swimming lessons.

127116.explanation: According to D66, hospitation rental is a simple and quick way to create more housing space. Students, starters, and young people can find a place faster, while older residents or single people gain extra income and companionship.

127119.explanation: We focus on car-reduced village centers. This means parking on the edges, with cyclists and pedestrians prioritized.

127121.explanation: A new theater is necessary due to the condition of the current Naald. Renovation will not solve the problems.

127123.explanation: This is not about more asphalt, but smarter use and expansion of the road network where necessary. This concerns areas with new residential neighborhoods or business parks. This does not require new roads, but smart adjustments to enable better traffic flow.

127126.explanation: There is no need for a new recycling center. In the long term, relocation may be desirable due to other spatial developments.

127127.explanation: With a growing population, facilities should also grow. This requires a sharp view on member growth in sports clubs, fitting within a planned approach according to the integrated sports housing plan.

127069.explanation: Residents managing, for example, a park is a good thing; it makes people responsible for their environment. It is community-building.

127071.explanation: It is important that restaurants close at a certain time, also to prevent noise nuisance.

127072.explanation: Sustainable energy is important. If residents collectively want to install a wind turbine (if space permits in the municipality), there could be consideration for municipal financial support.

127073.explanation: It must be carefully examined whether this is necessary and if it can be integrated into the environment.

127074.explanation: If there is sufficient support for this, it could be a good thing. Currently, the municipality of Westland only houses status holders.

127075.explanation: If residents experience nuisance such as odors and breathing difficulties (think of asthma patients), use should be discouraged under certain weather conditions.

127078.explanation: The municipality incurs costs for this; a personal contribution is acceptable.

127080.explanation: Accessibility of buildings for people with disabilities is important to us. Financial support could help implement this.

127082.explanation: We consider exercise and sports lessons more important. However, it might be worth considering offering free music lessons at school for one school year as a pilot.

127085.explanation: Recently, many enforcement officers (boa's) have been added in the municipality of Westland.

127086.explanation: One should be cautious with this, only if there is an urgent reason.

127087.explanation: This is better realized in a city.

127088.explanation: A baton should only be used to disperse people and only if really necessary.

127091.explanation: If offices remain empty for a long time, this is a possibility that should be examined.

127092.explanation: There are no universities of applied sciences or universities in Westland. Affordable rental and owner-occupied homes are more important than container homes.

127094.explanation: Residents with low incomes often already have difficulties. This offers some financial relief.

127095.explanation: However, what people need should always be carefully considered.

127096.explanation: It is important to see young people, especially those struggling or at risk of falling behind. Attention to this group can be helpful.

127098.explanation: Ideally, this would be a good thing. In residential neighborhoods, I think this is good, but across the entire built-up area is quite a lot...

127101.explanation: It is important that this is carefully considered and that people are treated respectfully.

127102.explanation: Price is important, but quality is more important.

127106.explanation: I believe there are already quite a few business parks in Westland. Each application should be carefully considered for necessity.

127108.explanation: Status holders should not remain indefinitely in shelters, which also incurs costs. There must be careful consideration of how to handle this. Residents of Westland also want a home.

127111.explanation: It is important that every child learns to swim.

127116.explanation: A maximum number of people allowed to live in a house should be set and enforced.

127119.explanation: In our opinion, there are enough parking spaces.

127121.explanation: ChristenUnie-SGP believes the current theater still suffices and does not need replacement. Proper maintenance is fine by us. Westland needs a small-scale theater.

127123.explanation: Good public transport should also be considered. Roads should be well maintained and widened if possible (if there is space).

127126.explanation: There are now two recycling centers, one in Naaldwijk and one in Monster. It's convenient that you can drop off waste at two locations.

127127.explanation: If many residents move in due to new construction in a municipality, it is important to assess whether expansion is necessary. Westland already has many sports parks.

127069.explanation: We applaud the self-management of certain public green spaces. However, self-management of a sports hall is undesirable given the complexity and risks involved. Providing good facilities for sports, exercise, and socializing remains an important task for the municipality.

127071.explanation: We relax opening hours and make terrace expansions easier so that residents can better respond to local demand and the village centers remain lively.

127072.explanation: Energy policy must be reliable and affordable above all.

127073.explanation: We focus on growth and retention of Westland companies and are critical of the establishment of large multinationals within our municipality.

127074.explanation: We do not allow shelter locations in order to protect the municipality against structural insecurity, nuisance, pollution, and pressure on our facilities.

127075.explanation: We will never agree to a ban on burning or prohibitions on wood stoves and other traditional heating methods.

127078.explanation: Bulky waste can be brought to one of the recycling centers or picked up for a small fee. This small fee is acceptable and we do not intend to make this free.

127080.explanation: The municipality should reduce regulatory pressure on the private sector, allowing entrepreneurs to make their buildings more accessible to people with disabilities themselves.

127082.explanation: The municipality should focus on its core tasks and although we consider music important, additional subsidies from the municipality are not desired.

127085.explanation: Fortunately, there has been an increase in the number of enforcement officers and police officers recently. However, we must prevent enforcement officers from receiving tasks or powers that belong exclusively to fully qualified police.

127086.explanation: We deploy cameras occasionally and temporarily at problem locations to prevent large-scale surveillance.

127087.explanation: We consider it important that people feel safe in their own homes and focus on social control. Cooperation with Veilig Thuis Haaglanden and the Social Core Team Westland is essential in this.

127088.explanation: Fortunately, there has been an increase in the number of enforcement officers and police officers recently. However, we must prevent enforcement officers from receiving tasks or powers that belong exclusively to fully qualified police.

127091.explanation: We facilitate developers who want to transform vacant commercial and office buildings into homes for Westland residents seeking housing. This prevents deterioration, makes use of existing structures, and provides quick housing alongside new construction.

127092.explanation: We support various conventional homes with a long lifespan, suitable for students, starters, families, and seniors.

127094.explanation: We strive for self-reliance of all our residents, with exceptions made for extreme cases. Currently, the standard has been lowered so much that too many municipal taxes are being waived. This must stop.

127095.explanation: We strive for self-reliance of all our residents, with exceptions made for extreme cases. The income limit must be maintained.

127096.explanation: We focus on efficiency, restrict drastic measures such as child removals to the absolute minimum, and ensure families receive timely and appropriate assistance.

127098.explanation: The trend toward a maximum speed limit of 30 km/h within built-up areas must be reversed. Roads should be realistically assessed for maximum speeds to prevent accidents and promote traffic flow.

127101.explanation: We are grateful for the professionalism, compassion, and dedication of those in healthcare. We currently face a shortage of healthcare personnel, placing extra burden on existing caregivers. We support less bureaucracy and restrictions so that more time can be devoted to providing care.

127102.explanation: We focus on efficiency, restrict drastic measures such as child removals to the absolute minimum, and ensure families receive timely and appropriate assistance.

127106.explanation: We modernize outdated industrial areas and improve their accessibility so that companies can remain and grow here.

127108.explanation: We ensure that Westlanders have the first opportunity for social rental and affordable homeownership. We completely abolish urgent status and priority schemes for status holders when allocating social rental homes.

127111.explanation: A distinctive feature of our municipality is the extensive network of waterways and the coast on the North Sea. Therefore, it is essential that sufficient facilities exist for learning to swim. We focus on preserving and expanding our existing swimming pools (for example, De Waterman swimming pool).

127116.explanation: We relax building and rental regulations to make it easier to create multi-generational homes, rent out rooms in a shared household, build extensions, dormers, and outbuildings on one’s own plot.

127119.explanation: We maintain free parking options such as at Willem van Hooffstraat (De Tuinen parking garage), Verdilaan (Albert Heijn), and Prins Bernhardstraat (Jumbo).

127121.explanation: We oppose the new construction of theater De Naald because the management's ambition does not align with the needs of the majority of our residents. We support preserving a modest, cozy, and intimate Westland theater with responsible policies for the future.

127123.explanation: We invest in good car access. Roads are actively maintained and expanded so that traffic can flow smoothly and safely.

127126.explanation: We ensure that Westlanders can always dispose of their waste for free at one of the recycling centers. We want a clean municipality where waste is professionally separated for recycling, reuse, or processing. We act strictly against illegal dumping of waste.

127127.explanation: Sports associations are an essential part of health and social cohesion. We cherish our sports clubs, and they must be able to grow.

127069.explanation: Provided this is done with passion and involvement. Possibly under municipal supervision.

127071.explanation: Clear guidelines that allow deviations per individual location to prevent nuisance.

127072.explanation: At a time when we need to increasingly focus on generating clean, sustainable energy, it is important that this is encouraged.

127073.explanation: Westland is now fully built up. Large distribution centers should be constructed outside built-up areas as much as possible.

127074.explanation: We do not oppose national guidelines. However, Westland has a significant shortage of suitable housing for starters and seniors. That must be addressed first.

127075.explanation: However, responsible use should be expected.

127078.explanation: This prevents littering and random dumping of waste.

127080.explanation: Entrepreneurs must ensure that everyone can access their buildings themselves.

127082.explanation: Musical and cultural development is important and may be supported with an individual subsidy.

127085.explanation: Safety is essential for the citizens.

127086.explanation: Safety for everyone is essential. Camera surveillance increases safety in public spaces.

127087.explanation: If necessary, we must help victims with this.

127088.explanation: The monopoly on violence lies with the police. Otherwise, make enforcement officers part of the police.

127091.explanation: The homes must be part of the housing corporations or the municipality.

127092.explanation: 50PLUS stands for lifelong accessible homes. However, renting rooms should be made more attractive for students.

127094.explanation: If someone is really in trouble, they must be able to receive help.

127095.explanation: Tailor-made solutions must be provided on a case-by-case basis, allowing deviation from a standard arrangement.

127096.explanation: Youth and elderly work must be encouraged.

127098.explanation: It must be assessed on location where this is necessary.

127101.explanation: Care providers deliver care to everyone, regardless of faith, culture, or beliefs.

127106.explanation: The allocation of space is now clear.

127108.explanation: 50PLUS believes that everyone is equal and should be treated accordingly.

127111.explanation: We are a water-rich municipality. Everyone should be able to swim.

127116.explanation: It must be possible to (temporarily) rent out vacant rooms to counter the housing shortage.

127119.explanation: There are two parking garages that are often far from full.

127121.explanation: Provided that local culture also has a place here.

127123.explanation: There is no space for this. 50PLUS strongly favors better public transport to relieve the roads (light rail).

127126.explanation: Naaldwijk has an excellent recycling center.

127127.explanation: Westland already has many large, beautiful sports parks.

statements.title: Westland should build more roads.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.

statements.theme: Closing times for bars and restaurants
statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Westland should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: Westland should expand the sports parks in the municipality.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
There should be more camera surveillance in Westland.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality must invest in temporary housing for students, for example container housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in tijdelijke huisvesting voor studenten, bijvoorbeeld

titleParts.glossary: Moveable and temporary homes, usually made from a shipping container.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Westland should have a women's shelter.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain permitted in Westland.

titleParts.text: In Westland moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on youth work.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
People and organizations that help young people in the municipality with various things.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: There should be more business parks in Westland.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The BOAs who enforce safety in Westland should be issued batons.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Westland controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: Westland should have a new recycling/household waste centre.

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the centre of Naaldwijk.
A new building should be constructed for Theater De Naald in Naaldwijk.
